response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and valuable IDE experience from their National Instruments role, there is a critical skill gap. The position requires 'strong expertise in Java and Kotlin' and the Spring ecosystem—neither of which are evident in the resume. Recent experience (2018-present) has focused on TypeScript/Node.js backends, away from JVM development entirely. The decade-old IDE work at NI, though relevant, is too distant and insufficient to compensate for the lack of current Java/Kotlin/Spring depth.
strengths: Direct IDE internals experience from National Instruments LabWindows/CVI development
Demonstrated commitment to software craftsmanship, testing culture, and code quality through multiple open-source testing frameworks
Proven ability to work on large-scale, complex systems including microservices handling millions of requests per second
concerns: No demonstrated expertise in Java or Kotlin—only sparse, undocumented mentions of Java in 2016-2018 roles; Kotlin not mentioned at all
Complete absence of Spring ecosystem experience (Spring, Spring Boot, Hibernate, etc.) despite being an explicit required qualification
Career trajectory over the past 7 years diverged toward TypeScript/Node.js backend and full-stack web development, away from JVM-focused tooling
